pages.full_text: [Glued-in illustrated map postcard of the Swiss Alps / Lake region, with the following printed labels visible on the left margin of the postcard: Mont Blanc, Grand Combin, Matterhorn, Monte Rosa, Bassodino. The map shows an aerial/relief view of the Alps with lakes and valleys. No handwritten text on this page.]
pages.notes: The entire left page is occupied by a glued-in illustrated relief/panorama map postcard of the Swiss Alps and Lake Lugano/Lake Maggiore region. Printed place names visible on the left spine label: Mont Blanc, Grand Combin, Matterhorn, Monte Rosa, Bassodino. No handwritten page number visible. No handwritten text on this page beyond a small blue bookmark tab at the top. The printed map labels are not handwritten diary text.

pages.full_text:
__July__ Wednesday
      Simon was unwell in the night, dizzys; fever, aching. In the morning he pottered off for a walk. There was a magnificent waterfall + very nice unobtrusive well-designed modern house. Thomas also walked.
      We packed up at lunch time + set off for __Lugano__. It's a very pretty town with a tree park + playground. We looked at large Swatches + bought icecreams in a piazza (£25) + went out in 2 paddle boats. After we bumped, Charles cried the rest of the time because he thought they were going to sink.
      We went on south + crossed into Italy. Apalling motor bikes. Lovely houses.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '11' visible in top-right corner. 'July' is underlined as part of the heading. 'Lugano' is underlined in the text. 'dizzys' — likely meant 'dizzies' or 'dizziness'; transcribed verbatim. 'Apalling' — diary spelling preserved (one 'p'). Year cannot be confirmed from this page; the mention of Lugano and crossing into Italy places this on a Swiss/Italian summer trip, but no year is given. The heading 'July Wednesday' provides no year anchor. 'piazza (£25)' — the £25 likely refers to total cost of icecreams and paddle boats. The final text is cut off at the bottom-right of the image; 'Lovely houses.' may continue on the next page. 'bumped' — refers to the paddle boats bumping into each other.
